subject: [PATCH v5 1/2] fs/proc/task_mmu: fix PAGEMAP_SCAN written state for PMD holes

2026-07-15.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the PAGEMAP_SCAN functionality of the Linux kernel, specifically when dealing with PMD holes in uffd-wp VMAs. The bug causes write-protected ranges to be reported as not written, leading to data corruption during live migration or restore operations. The patch corrects this by reporting PMD holes in non-hugetlb uffd-wp VMAs as written, matching the existing behavior for pte_none handling. This fix is necessary to prevent data corruption and ensure accurate tracking of write-protected ranges.

2026-07-16.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the PAGEMAP_SCAN functionality of the Linux kernel, specifically when dealing with PMD holes (huge page table entries) in uffd-wp VMAs (userfaultfd write-protect). The issue arises from pagemap_scan_pte_hole() skipping holes that are not populated with page tables. The patch corrects this by treating unpopulated slots as written, matching the handling of pte_none() in pagemap_page_category(). This ensures that a missing marker in an uffd-wp VMA indicates that the range was cleared via MADV_DONTNEED, preventing data corruption during restore or live migration. The patch excludes hugetlb entries, which are handled differently and do not require this fix.
